description: Recto: Two lines of documentary Arabic text with wide line spacing. Medieval-era. Reused on recto and verso for Seliḥōt li-Yeme Teshūva. The verso may be a title page for the liturgical work and the recto bears the name Yaʿaqov ha-[Kohe]n(?) b. Shelomo, which could be an ownership note or scribal attribution.
